column1: m2.1  As a User, I want to log in via Email Address and password with OTP security

column2: Verify the Login page UI elements
column4: 1. The mobile app is launched.
column5:
1. Tap the "Sign In" button to go to the login page
2. Compare the displayed UI elements (layout, fonts, buttons icons, colors, fonts, spacing, and labels) to the Figma mockup.
column6:
1. The screen should display fields for username/email and password.
2. The "Sign In"  and "Back" button should be present.
3. There should be clear and user-friendly labels for all input fields.
4. "Forgot your password?" should be displayed below the input fields.
5. All UI elements should match the Figma design mockup in terms of layout, fonts, buttons icons, colors, fonts, spacing, and labels.

Refer to Card Bank Login page in this Figma file.

column2: Verify Password Masking and Eye Icon Toggle Functionality

column2: Verify if user can login with valid credentials and OTP
column4:
1. The mobile app is launched.

5. Tap on "Sign In" button
6. On the OTP screen, enter the OTP sent to the registered email address.
column6:

2. The user should be able to enter OTP and be redirected to the landing/home page.

column2: Verify is user can login with valid credentials using saved password
column4:
1. The mobile banking app is installed and launched.
2. The user has previously saved their login credentials via the device's password manager (e.g., iCloud Keychain, Google Smart Lock).
3. The user has successfully logged in and verified their identity at least once.
column5:
1. Launch the mobile banking app.
2. On the login screen, tap the email input field.
3. Tap the "Passwords" or key icon to open the device’s saved credentials list.
4. Select the correct saved credentials for the banking app.
5. The app auto-fills the email and password fields.
6. Tap the "Sign In" button.
7. On the OTP screen, enter the valid OTP sent to the registered email or mobile number.
column6:
1. The system successfully processes the credentials and navigates to the OTP verification screen.
2. Upon entering a valid OTP, the user is logged in and redirected to the home page.

column2: Verify that the user cannot request a new OTP upon login before the 1-minute cooldown period has passed

3. The cooldown timer should continue until it completes the full 1-minute duration.

column2: Verify that the user can request a new OTP upon login

column2: Verify if previously received OTP is not valid upon login
column4:
1. The mobile app is launched.
2. The user is on the OTP screen after entering valid email and password.
column5:

2. Check the received email to confirm a new OTP has been sent.
3. Attempt to enter the previously received OTP.
4. Observe the system response.
5. Repeat steps 1-4.
column6:
1. The system should display a validation message: 

“OTP is either Invalid or has expired, Please try again or request a new OTP”

2. Every request of new OTP will trigger all previously requested OTP to be invalid.

column2: Verify validation on the 5th failed OTP attempt (triggering max allowed attempts error)
column4:
1. The mobile app is launched.
2. The user is on the login page.
4. A valid email and password are known.
5. The system allows a maximum of 5 OTP attempts per session.
column5:
1. Tap on the email input field.
2. Enter a valid email address.
3. Tap on the password input field.
4. Enter a valid password.
5. Tap on the "Sign In" button.
6. On the OTP screen, enter an invalid OTP for 4 times
7. On the 5th attempt, enter an invalid OTP again.
8. Observe the system behavior immediately after the 5th invalid entry.
9. Click" Ok"
column6:
1. Immediately after the 5th invalid OTP attempt, the system should display:
"Verification Failed: You have reached the maximum number of attempts. For your security, we’re redirecting you to the previous page."

2. The user should be redirected back to the login screen.
column15: Tagged Manual/Skipped — requires magic OTP disabled in test environment

column2: Verify validation on 5th OTP input attempt across multiple resend requests
column4:
1. The mobile app is launched.
2. The user is on the login page.
3. A valid email and password are known.
4. The system allows only 5 total OTP input attempts per session, regardless of resend count.
5. The user has reached the OTP verification screen during login.
column5:
1. Enter 2 invalid OTPs (Attempts 1 & 2).
2. Tap "Resend code".
3. Enter 2 more invalid OTPs (Attempts 3 & 4).
4. Tap "Resend code" again.
5. Enter one more invalid OTP (Attempt 5).
6. Observe the system behavior immediately after the 5th invalid entry.
7. Click" Ok"
column6:
1. Immediately after the 5th invalid OTP attempt, the system should display:

"Verification Failed: You have reached the maximum number of attempts. For your security, we’re redirecting you to the previous page."

column2:
Verify validation when OTP session expires before max attempts 

(When the User has reached Session ID timeout without reaching the max input attempts.)
column4:
1. User has attempted to login and OTP session started
2. User is on the OTP verification screen.
column5:
1. Within 5 mins, input invalid OTP for 3 times
2. After 5 mins when the OTP session expires, regardless of validity input an OTP (4th attempt)
3. Tap on "Resend Code"
4. Tap on "Request New Code" button.
column6:
1. On the 4th OTP input attempt after session expiry, the system should display:

“OTP is either invalid or has expired. Please try again or request a new OTP.”

2. After tapping the "Resend Code" link, the system should show:

“OTP Expired: Your one-time password has expired. Request a new code to continue.”

3. After tapping the "Request New Code" button:

- The user should be redirected back to the login screen.

- A new OTP session should be required to continue the login process.

column2: Verify that the system displays the correct error modal when an invalid or valid OTP is entered after the 5-minute OTP validity window has expired.
column4:
1. User is on the OTP verification screen.

3. The 5-minute OTP validity window has expired.
column5:
1. Wait until the OTP validity period exceeds 5 minutes.
2. Enter a valid or invalid OTP.
3. Observe the system behavior.
4. Tap the OK button on the modal.
column6:
1. System does not proceed with OTP verification.
2. An error modal is displayed with the message:
“OTP is either invalid or has expired. Please try again or request a new OTP.”
3. The modal displays an OK button.
4. Tapping OK redirects the user to the previous screen.

column2: Verify that the user's email is blocked for 30 minutes after 3 unverified login sessions with 5 invalid attempts per session
column4:
1.User is registered.
2. Email is not currently blocked.
column5:
1. Initiate Login Session #1
 - Enter 5 incorrect OTP(s)
2. Initiate Login Session #2
 - Enter 5 incorrect OTP(s)
3. Initiate Login Session #3
 - Enter 5 incorrect OTP(s)

4. Attempt to initiate Login Session #4 using the same email.
column6:
1. After the 3rd unverified login session, the system blocks the user's email for 30 minutes.

2. Any OTP login attempt during the block period should return an error message:

"You have exceeded the maximum number of OTP attempts. You can try again in <time remaining> minutes."

3. Email is sent to the customer:
Subject: Security Notice: Unusual Activity Detected on Your Account

Body:
Hi <Customer Name>,

We noticed several attempts to access your account that exceeded our security limits. To help keep your account safe, we’ve temporarily locked it as a precaution.

If these attempts were made by you, no further action is needed. You can try signing in again after the cooldown period. If you need immediate access, please contact <replace customer support email> for assistance with resetting your account.

Subject: Security Notice: Unusual Activity Detected on Your Account

Hi <Customer Name>,

If these attempts were made by you, no further action is needed. You can try signing in again after the cooldown period. If you need immediate access, please contact <replace customer support email> for assistance with resetting your account.

column2: Verify that a user receives a blocking error message when trying to log in with an email address that is currently blocked.
column4: 1. The user's email has been blocked due to failed OTP attempts
column5:
1. During the 30-minute blocking period, attempt to log in using the blocked email address multiple times.
2. Observe the system’s response.
column6:
1. System does not proceed with OTP generation.

2. A clear error message is displayed indicating the email is blocked:

"You have exceeded the maximum number of OTP attempts. You can try again in <time remaining> minutes."

3. Each time the user attempts to log in while the email is still blocked, the system displays the same error message, with the remaining block time updated accordingly.

column2: Verify that a user can log in successfully with a valid OTP after the 30-minute block period has expired.

2. Attempt to log in using the same email.
3. Enter the correct OTP.
column6:
1. Login process resumes normally.
2. OTP is sent and validated successfully.
3. User is logged in.

column2: Verify that the system does not block the user if they input a valid OTP on the 5th attempt during the 3rd session
column4:
1. User is registered.
2. Two previous login sessions have already occurred, both unverified and/or expired.
5. Each session allows up to 5 OTP attempts.
column5:
1. Initiate Login Session #3
2. Enter 4 incorrect OTPs.
3. On the 5th attempt, enter the correct OTP.
4. Observe the outcome of the session.
5. Attempt to initiate Login Session #4.
column6:
1. Session #3 is considered verified.
2. Blocking is not triggered (since only 2 sessions were unverified in last 15 minutes).
3. Session #4 starts normally.
4. User is not blocked.

column2: Verify that the counter resets after successful verification of any session
column4:
1. User is registered and not currently blocked.
2. Each OTP session allows a maximum of 5 attempts.
3. Session timeout is 5 minutes.
4. Blocking occurs only after 3 unverified sessions within 15 minutes.
5. Two previous login sessions have already occurred, both unverified and/or expired.
column5:
1. Initiate Login Session #3
2. Enter 4 incorrect OTPs.
3. On the 5th attempt, enter the correct OTP.
4. Observe the outcome of the session.
5. 1. Initiate Login Session #4
 - Leave the OTP input field blank
 - Cancel the OTP verification and go back to login page.

2. Initiate Login Session #5
 - Leave the OTP input field blank
 - Cancel the OTP verification and go back to login page.

3. Initiate Login Session #6
 - Leave the OTP input field blank
 - Cancel the OTP verification and go back to login page.

4. Attempt to initiate Login Session #7 using the same email."
column6:
1. Login Sessions 4-6 are counted as new failures.
2. No blocking should occur on session #6 because the previous two failures (1 & 2) were cleared due to the successful verification in #3.
3. Blocking would only occur if 3 new unverified sessions happen after a successful one - within a new 15-minute window so in this case on Session 7
4. On Session 7, a clear error message is displayed indicating the email is blocked:

"You have exceeded the maximum number of OTP attempts. You can try again in <time remaining> minutes."

column2: Verify that the system does not block the user if the 3 unverified sessions span more than 15 minutes
column4:
1.User is registered.
2. Email is not currently blocked.
3. Blocking occurs only after 3 unverified sessions within 15 minutes.
column5:
1. Start Login Session #1
 - Time: 00:00:00
 - Enter incorrect OTPs (1–5) or let session expire unverified.
 - Ends at: 00:05:00 min

2. Start Login Session #2
 - Time: 00:06:00 min
 - Request OTP.
 - Enter incorrect OTPs (1–5) or let session expire unverified.
 - Ends at: 00:10:00 min

3. Wait for 5 mins
 - Time: 00:10:00 min
 - Ends at 00:15:00 min

4. Start Login Session #3
- Time: 00:16:00 min
- Enter incorrect OTPs (1–5) or let session expire unverified.
- Ends at: 00:20:00 mins

5. Start Login Session #4
 - Time: 00:20:00 or 00:21:00  min
 - Attempt to initiate login using the same email.
column6:
1. The system does not block the user.

2. The system evaluates the previous sessions:

 - Session #1: ended at 00:05

 - Session #3: started at 00:16 → more than 15 minutes since #1 ended

Therefore, the 3 unverified sessions do not fall within a 15-minute window.

3. Session #4 is allowed.

4. OTP is sent normally.

5. No blocking error is shown.

column2: Verify account is blocked after 5 consecutive failed login attempts
column4:
1. User is on the login page.

column5: 1. Enter incorrect password 5 times consecutively.
column6:
1. On the 5th failed attempt, system blocks the account.
2. Displays message: “Incorrect email or password. Too many attempts -- please try again in 5 minutes.”
3. All further login attempts denied during cooldown.
4. Email is sent to the customer. Subject: Security Alert: Your Account Has Been Temporarily Locked.
Email body:
Body:
Hi <Customer Name>,

For your protection, we temporarily locked your account after multiple incorrect password attempts. This may happen if:

    - You forgot your password, or
    - Someone else tried to access your account without permission

To regain access, simply use the Forgot Password option in the mobile app.

If you did not try to log in, please contact support@higala.ph immediately so we can help secure your account.

column2:
Verify validation when OTP session expires before max attempts 

(When the User has reached Session ID timeout without reaching the max input attempts.)
column4:
1. The mobile app is launched.

3.The system allows a maximum of 5 OTP attempts per session.
column5:

2. After 5 mins when the OTP session expires, regardless of validity input an OTP (4th attempt)
3. Tap on "Resend Code"
4. Tap on "Request New Code" button.
column6:
1. On the 4th OTP input attempt after session expiry, the system should display:

“OTP is either invalid or has expired. Please try again or request a new OTP.”

2. After tapping the "Resend Code" link, the system should show:

“OTP Expired: Your one-time password has expired. Request a new code to continue.”

3. After tapping the "Request New Code" button:

- The user should be redirected back to entering the email address page. 
- A new OTP session should be required to continue the login process.

column2: Verify that the user's email is blocked for 60 minutes after 3 unverified Forgot Password OTP sessions with 5 invalid attempts per session
column4:
1.User is registered.
2. Email is not currently blocked.
column5:
1. Initiate Forgot Password Session #1
 - Enter 5 incorrect OTP(s)
2. Initiate Forgot Password Session #2
 - Enter 5 incorrect OTP(s)
3. Initiate Forgot Password Session #3
 - Enter 5 incorrect OTP(s)

4. Attempt to initiate Forgot Password Session #4
5. Click "OK"
column6:
1. After the 3rd unverified OTP session, if all 3 occurred within 15 minutes, the system blocks the user's email for 60 minutes.

2. After the 3rd unverified session, an error message is displayed:
"You have exceeded the maximum number of OTP attempts. You can try again in 59 mins."

3. Any attempt in clicking "Submit" from the Forgot Password page during the block period should return an error message:

"You have exceeded the maximum number of OTP attempts. You can try again in <time remaining> minutes."

4. Clicking "OK" will close the modal and user is still on the Forgot Password page

5. Email is sent to the customer:
Subject: Security Notice: Unusual Activity Detected on Your Account

Body:
Hi <Customer Name>,

We noticed several attempts to access your account that exceeded our security limits. To help keep your account safe, we’ve temporarily locked it as a precaution.

If these attempts were made by you, no further action is needed. You can try signing in again after the cooldown period. If you need immediate access, please contact <replace customer support email> for assistance with resetting your account.

column2: Verify that the system does not block the user if they input a valid OTP on the 5th attempt during the 3rd Forgot Password OTP  session
column4:
1. User is registered.
2. Two previous forgot password sessions have already occurred, both unverified and/or expired.
5. Each session allows up to 5 OTP attempts.
column5:
1. Initiate forgot password Session #3
2. Enter 4 incorrect OTPs.
3. On the 5th attempt, enter the correct OTP.
4. Observe the outcome of the session.
5. Attempt to initiate forgot password Session #4.
column6:
1. Session #3 is considered verified.
2. Blocking is not triggered (since only 2 sessions were unverified in last 10 minutes).
3. Session #4 starts normally.
4. User is not blocked.

column2: Verify that the system does not block the user if the 3 unverified Forgot Password OTP sessions span more than 15 minutes
column4:
1.User is registered.
2. Email is not currently blocked.
3. Blocking occurs only after 3 unverified sessions within 10 minutes.
column5:
1. Start Session #1
 - Time: 00:00:00
 - Enter 2 incorrect OTPs then exit
 - Ends at: 00:05:00 min

2. Start  Session #2
 - Time: 00:05:00 min
 - Request OTP.
 - Enter 2 incorrect OTPs then exit
 - Ends at: 00:10:00 min

3. Wait for 5 mins
 - Time: 00:10:00 min
 - Ends at 00:15:00 min

4. Start Session #3
- Time: 00:16:00 min
- Enter incorrect OTPs then exit.
- Ends at: 00:20:00 mins

5. Start Session #4
 - Time: 00:20:00 or 00:21:00  min
 - Attempt to initiate send money session using the same email.
column6:
1. The system does not block the user.

2. The system evaluates the previous sessions:

 - Session #1: ended at 00:05

 - Session #3: started at 00:16 → more than 15 minutes since #1 ended

Therefore, the 3 unverified sessions do not fall within a 15-minute window.

3. Session #4 is allowed.

4. OTP is sent normally.

5. No blocking error is shown.

column2: Verify that the counter resets after successful verification of any forgot password OTP session
column4:
1. User is registered and not currently blocked.
2. Each OTP session allows a maximum of 5 attempts.
3. Session timeout is 5 minutes.
4. Blocking occurs only after 3 unverified sessions within 15 minutes.
5. Two previous send money sessions have already occurred, both unverified and/or expired.
column5:
1. Initiate forgot password Session #3
2. Enter 4 incorrect OTPs.
3. On the 5th attempt, enter the correct OTP.
4. Observe the outcome of the session.
5. Initiate forgot password Session #4
 - Leave the OTP input field blank
 - Cancel the OTP verification 

2.  Initiate forgot password Session #5
 - Leave the OTP input field blank
 - Cancel the OTP verification 

3.  Initiate forgot password Session #6
 - Leave the OTP input field blank
 - Cancel the OTP verification 

4. Attempt to initiate Forgot Password Session #7 using the same email."
column6:
1. Forgot Password OTP sessions 4-6 are counted as new failures.
2. No blocking should occur on session #6 because the previous two failures (1 & 2) were cleared due to the successful verification in #3.
3. Blocking would only occur if 3 new unverified sessions happen after a successful one - within a new 15-minute window so in this case on Session 7
4. On Session 7, a clear error message is displayed indicating the email is blocked:

"You have exceeded the maximum number of OTP attempts. You can try again in <time remaining> minutes."
